originalTitle: Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict
title: Big News from Ethiopia! A Leader Gets Lots of Votes!
content:
Hi Friends! Exciting News from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly African news reporter here, ready to share some exciting news from our amazing continent! Today, we're flying all the way to a wonderful country called Ethiopia. Can you say 'Ethiopia'? It's a special place in East Africa. Ethiopia is a very special country with so many beautiful things, like tall mountains and amazing animals. It's also where yummy coffee first came from! Isn't that cool? Guess what? Something really big just happened there! The grown-ups in Ethiopia chose their leader again. And one leader, named Abiy Ahmed, and his team, won by a whole lot of votes! It means many, many people want him to continue leading their country.

What is an Election?

So, what is an election? It's like a really important game where everyone gets to pick a team. But instead of picking a sports team, people pick who they want to help run their country. It's how people choose their government. Imagine you have a big class, and you need to pick a class captain. Everyone gets to say who they think would be best. That's a little bit like an election! In an election, grown-ups go to special places to put their vote. A vote is like saying 'I choose this person!' or 'I choose this group!' It's their way of having a say. Why do we have elections? So that everyone has a voice! It means the country is run by people chosen by the people who live there. It's a fair way to decide. Did you know? Elections happen in many countries all over the world, not just in Africa! They are a way for people to choose their government peacefully.

Meet Abiy Ahmed, the Leader!

The person who won a lot of votes in Ethiopia is called Abiy Ahmed. He is the Prime Minister of Ethiopia. A Prime Minister is like the main leader of the country, who helps make important decisions. He has a party, which is like a team of people who work together. His team is called the Prosperity Party. They all want to make Ethiopia a better place for everyone who lives there. Abiy Ahmed has been the leader before. And now, because so many people voted for him and his team, he will be the leader again. This is very big news for Ethiopia! Did you know? Abiy Ahmed won a very special prize called the Nobel Peace Prize a few years ago! He won it because he tried very hard to make peace with a neighbour country, Eritrea. That's a very kind and brave thing to do, isn't it? Trying to be friends instead of fighting.

Winning Big! What Does it Mean?

When we say Abiy Ahmed's party won by a 'landslide' or got a 'huge majority,' it means they got lots and lots more votes than anyone else. They were very popular! It's like if you were counting your toy cars, and you had 100 red cars and only 10 blue cars. You have a huge majority of red cars! That's how many more votes his team got. So, many, many people in Ethiopia chose his team. This means they trust him and his team to help lead the country. It shows they believe in his plans to make things better. This is important because when a leader has lots of support, it can make it easier for them to do good things for the country. Like building new schools, making sure everyone has enough food, or helping to create jobs for grown-ups.

Ethiopia is a Special Place!

Let's talk more about Ethiopia! It's not just a country; it's a land full of wonders. It's one of the oldest countries in the whole wide world! It has a very long and interesting history. Did you know? Ethiopia is home to some amazing animals you can only find there! Like the Gelada Baboon, which looks like it has a red heart on its chest, and the shy Ethiopian Wolf. They are very special creatures! The people of Ethiopia are known for their warm smiles and rich culture. They have beautiful traditional clothes and delicious food. They are very proud of their heritage. And remember that coffee we talked about? Legend says a goat herder in Ethiopia first discovered coffee beans many, many years ago when his goats got super energetic after eating them! He shared his discovery, and now we all get to enjoy coffee. Ethiopia also has famous churches carved right into rocks, called the Lalibela churches. They are amazing to see and are thousands of years old!

Some Challenges and Hopes for the Future

Now, even when a leader wins by a lot, sometimes there are still some challenges. It's like in a game, even if your team is winning, there might be tricky parts or problems to solve. The news says there have been some 'unrest' in different parts of Ethiopia. This means some people are not happy, and sometimes there can be arguments or even a little bit of fighting in certain areas. It's sad when people are not getting along. It also talks about 'tensions with its neighbours.' Neighbours are other countries that are close to Ethiopia. Sometimes countries have arguments, just like sometimes friends have arguments. They might disagree on things. It's important for everyone to talk and try to understand each other. When people talk kindly and listen, they can often find a way to be friends again and solve their problems peacefully. The hope is that with a strong leader chosen by many people, Ethiopia can work towards peace. Peace means everyone is safe, happy, and can live together without fighting. It's a very important goal. Abiy Ahmed and his team will now work hard to make sure everyone in Ethiopia is safe and happy. They will try to solve problems inside the country and make friends with their neighbours. It's a big job! Did you know? When countries are at peace, they can help each other! They can trade things, share ideas, and even visit each other easily. Peace is good for everyone and helps countries grow stronger. So, this big win for Abiy Ahmed means he has a chance to help bring more peace and good times to Ethiopia. We hope for the best for all the wonderful people there! Keep shining, Ethiopia!
summary: Abiy Ahmed's party won the election in Ethiopia with many votes, and they will continue to lead the country, facing challenges but hoping for peace.
whyMatters: This is important because the people of Ethiopia chose who they want to lead them, and the new leader will try to bring peace and good things to everyone in the country, even when there are some challenges to fix.

originalTitle: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
title: Time to Say Sorry! African and Caribbean Friends Talk About History!
content:
Hello, My Amazing Friends!

Hi everyone! Your friendly African news reporter is here! Today, we're going to talk about something very important. It's about our past and making things right for the future. Leaders from many countries in Africa and the Caribbean are speaking up. They are asking for something special. They want other countries to say "sorry" for a very sad thing that happened a long, long time ago.

What Was "Transatlantic Slavery"?

This is a big word, but let's make it simple. Many, many years ago, people from some countries did a very bad thing. They sailed to Africa and took people from their homes. They forced these people onto big, crowded ships. These ships then sailed across the huge Atlantic Ocean. The journey was long and terrible. Many people got sick, and some even died on the way. Imagine being taken from your family and home, far, far away. Once they arrived, usually in places like the Caribbean islands or America, these African people were made to work for free. They were treated very, very badly. They had to work in fields or houses, from morning until night, without any pay. This was called "slavery." It means people were owned by other people, like objects. They had no freedom. They couldn't choose where to live, what to do, or even who to be with. This sad time lasted for hundreds of years. It caused a lot of pain and suffering for millions of people. **Did you know?** The Atlantic Ocean is the second biggest ocean on Earth! It connects Africa to the Americas.

Who Are These Leaders?

Leaders are like the captains of a country. They help make important decisions for everyone who lives there. These leaders come from many countries in Africa. Africa is a very big continent with lots of different countries and cultures. They also come from countries in the Caribbean. The Caribbean is a group of beautiful islands with sunny beaches and clear blue water. These islands are close to North and South America. Many people living in the Caribbean today have ancestors – that means family from a long, long time ago – who came from Africa. They were taken there during the time of slavery. So, these leaders are standing together, like a big family, speaking with one voice. **Did you know?** Africa is a huge continent with 54 countries! The Caribbean has many islands, some are countries too, like Jamaica and Barbados.

Asking for "Sorry" (A Formal Apology)

Imagine you accidentally break your friend's favorite toy. What do you do? You say "sorry," right? Saying sorry shows you understand you made a mistake. It shows you care about how your friend feels. It helps to mend hearts. These leaders are asking for a special kind of "sorry." They call it a "formal apology." This means they want the countries that did the bad things long ago to officially stand up and say, "We are sorry for what happened." It's important because it acknowledges the terrible pain and suffering that happened. It helps heal old wounds that are still felt today. It shows respect for the people who suffered and their families. It's the first step to making things better. **Did you know?** The word "apology" comes from an old Greek word meaning "a speech in defense." But now it means saying sorry for something wrong you've done!

Asking for Help with Money (Debt Relief)

Sometimes, countries need to borrow money. Just like grown-ups might borrow money to buy a house or build a business. When countries borrow money, they have to pay it back. This is called "debt." It can be very hard for some countries to pay back all their debt, especially if they are still struggling from the past. The leaders are asking for "debt relief." This means they want some of the money they owe to be reduced. Or maybe, in some cases, they don't have to pay it back at all. This would help these countries have more money. They could use that money for important things like building new schools, better hospitals, clean water, and good things for all their people. They believe that the past struggles from slavery made it harder for their countries to grow rich and strong. **Did you know?** Many countries borrow money from big banks or other rich countries to build new roads, improve farms, or develop their towns and cities.

Asking for Money for the Past (Financial Compensation)

This is another very important thing the leaders are asking for. Imagine someone took your favorite toys and broke them. Then, they used parts of your toys, or even the idea of your toys, to build their own super cool, giant toy that made them very rich. You would want them to give you something back, right? Maybe new toys, or money to buy new toys. "Financial compensation" means giving money or other things of value. It's to make up for the harm that was done. During slavery, people were forced to work for free, for hundreds of years. The countries and people who enslaved them got very, very rich from their hard work. They built their own countries stronger and wealthier. The leaders are saying that this money should help the countries that suffered. It's a way to try and balance things out a little bit. It's not about making anyone sad again. It's about making sure the future is fair for everyone and helps communities that were hurt by the past to grow strong. **Did you know?** The slave trade made a lot of money for some people, building big businesses like sugar plantations and cotton farms in other parts of the world.

Why Is This Important Now?

You might wonder why they are talking about something that happened so long ago. Even though it was a very long time ago, the effects are still felt today. Some countries are still poorer because of it. Some people still feel the sadness and unfairness because of how their ancestors were treated. Talking about it helps us all learn from history. It teaches us to be kind, fair, and respectful to everyone, no matter where they come from or what they look like. It's about building a better world where everyone is treated with dignity. It's about justice. Justice means treating everyone fairly and doing what is right when wrongs have been done. By talking about this, we can make sure such bad things never happen again. **Did you know?** The transatlantic slave trade went on for about 400 years! That's like from when your great-great-great-great-grandparents were born all the way until today!

What Happens Next?

The leaders have spoken loudly and clearly. Now, the countries they are asking to say "sorry" need to listen. They need to think about what the leaders have asked for. It might take some time, like when you ask for a special treat or a big decision needs to be made. But by talking about it, they are making a very important step. They are showing that everyone deserves to be treated well and that history matters. And that remembering our history helps us make a brighter, fairer future for all children, everywhere! Hooray for fairness and kindness! Thank you for listening, my friends!
summary: Leaders from African and Caribbean countries are asking for formal apologies, help with money (debt relief), and financial compensation from countries that benefited from the historical transatlantic slave trade.
whyMatters: This is important because it's about making things fair after a very sad and unfair part of history, helping countries grow, and teaching everyone to treat each other with kindness and respect today.

originalTitle: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
title: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
content: More than eight million South Africans are living with HIV – the highest number of any country in the world.

originalTitle: Europe heatwave scorches cities and wildlife
title: Europe Gets Super Hot! A Big Heatwave Story!
content:
Hello, Little Explorers!

Hi everyone! Your friendly news reporter from Africa is here. Today, I have a story from a faraway land called Europe. Europe is very far from us, across the big blue ocean. Imagine a place with lots of old castles, tall mountains, and pretty rivers. But something really unusual and hot happened there recently. It was like the sun decided to get super close to the Earth, making everything sizzle! This story is about a "heatwave." Have you ever felt a very, very hot day? A day when you just want to drink cool water and sit in the shade? Well, in Europe, it was like that for many, many days in a row! It was super-duper hot, even hotter than some summer days we have here sometimes. The sun shone so brightly, and the air felt like a warm blanket.

What is a Heatwave?

So, what is a heatwave? It's when the weather gets super hot, not just for one day, but for many days in a row. It’s like the sun decides to throw a big, long party, and everyone gets really warm! The air gets hot, the ground gets hot, and even the houses feel hot. When we say "scorches," it means it gets so hot that things can feel like they are burning, or drying out very fast. The ground can get super dry, and plants might start to droop. Imagine your favourite playground slide on a very sunny day. It gets too hot to touch, right? A heatwave makes lots of things feel like that, for a very long time. People need to be extra careful to stay cool and drink lots of water so they don't get sick from the heat.

Europe Felt Like a Giant Oven!

The news from Europe told us it was so hot, it felt like a giant oven! In some places, the air was warmer than 40 degrees Celsius. Wow! That’s like when the water in your bath is just right, but imagine the *air* being that warm! When it's that hot, it's really hard to play outside. **Did you know?** The highest temperature ever recorded on Earth was in Death Valley, USA, and it was 56.7 degrees Celsius! That is incredibly hot! Luckily, Europe did not get *that* hot, but it was still very unusual and uncomfortable for them. People who live in Europe are not used to such extreme heat, unlike some parts of Africa that are used to warm weather.

Cities Feeling the Heat

Europe has many big cities, with tall buildings and busy roads. When the heatwave came, these cities felt the heat even more. The sun shone down on all the concrete roads and brick buildings. Concrete and bricks love to soak up the sun's warmth, like a sponge soaking up water. Then, they let that heat out slowly, even at night! So, cities stayed hot all day and all night. People had to be very careful. They tried to stay inside where it was cooler. They drank lots and lots of water, more than usual. Some people went to special cooling centers, which are places with air conditioning to help everyone stay cool. Air conditioning is like a magic machine that makes the air cold! **Did you know?** Dark colours absorb more sunlight, making things hotter! That’s why many people wear light-coloured clothes on sunny days. Cities often have lots of dark roads and roofs, which can make them even hotter during a heatwave. This is called the "urban heat island" effect.

Animals Need Help Too!

It wasn't just people who felt the heat. Our animal friends also had a very tough time! Just like us, animals need water to drink when it's hot. But sometimes, their usual water holes or ponds dry up because of the intense heat. Birds found it hard to fly for a long time. Fish in rivers and lakes struggled because the water got too warm. Hedgehogs, deer, and foxes looked for shade and water, just like we do. Some animals even became sick because they couldn't find enough to drink or a cool place to rest. And guess what? When the ground and plants get very, very dry from the heat, it can be easy for fires to start. These are called bushfires or wildfires. These fires are very scary and dangerous for animals and people. They can burn down big forests, taking away the homes of many animals. Firefighters had to work super hard to put out these fires. **Did you know?** You can help animals during hot weather by leaving out a shallow dish of fresh water in your garden for birds, insects, and small animals to drink from. Just make sure it’s clean water every day!

Why Is Our Earth Getting So Hot?

You might be thinking, "Why is this happening, Mr. Reporter?" Well, our Earth is like a big home for all of us – people, animals, and plants. And sometimes, our home gets a little warmer than it should. Scientists, who are like super-smart detectives, tell us that the Earth is slowly getting warmer and warmer. This is called "climate change." Things we do, like driving cars that use fuel or making electricity in ways that send special gases into the air, can make our Earth warmer. It’s like putting too many blankets on when you're sleeping – you get too hot! When the Earth gets warmer, we can see more heatwaves, like the one in Europe, and other changes in the weather.

What Can We Do?

Even though Europe is far away, we all live on the same planet Earth. What happens there can remind us that we all need to take care of our home. So, what can we do, even as kids? We can try to save electricity by turning off lights when we leave a room. We can walk or ride bicycles instead of always using cars, if it’s safe. We can help plant trees, because trees are like big green superheroes that help clean the air and make things cooler. Every little bit helps! When we take care of our environment here in Africa, we are helping the whole world. We are making sure our beautiful nature, animals, and people stay safe and healthy.

Why This Matters to Us in Africa

You might wonder why a heatwave in Europe matters to us here in Africa. Well, we share one big planet! The air and oceans connect us all. When the weather changes in one part of the world, it can sometimes affect other parts too. For example, some parts of Africa also experience very hot weather, and sometimes heatwaves. Learning about Europe's heatwave helps us understand how important it is to be ready for hot days and to take care of our planet. It reminds us to look after our own environment, to plant trees, and to save water. We want our animals to be safe and our cities to be comfortable too. We are all part of the big Earth family!

Let's Be Kind to Our Planet!

So, remember the story of the super-hot Europe. It teaches us to be kind to our planet. Let's make sure we keep our Earth happy and healthy, so everyone – people, animals, and plants – can live safely and comfortably. Thank you for listening, little explorers! Keep exploring and keep caring!
summary: A very big heatwave made parts of Europe super hot, causing problems for people and animals, and reminding us to take care of our Earth.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how weather can change and affect everyone, everywhere, and teaches us that we all need to help keep our planet healthy.

originalTitle: Spain thrash Saudi Arabia 4-0 as Lamine Yamal off mark at World Cup 2026
title: Super Goals! Spain Wins Big in World Cup Fun!
content:
Hello, Super Football Fans!

Hello, super football fans! It's your kid-friendly reporter here. I'm bringing you exciting news from the world of football! Do you love watching people kick a ball? Do you love seeing goals? Well, something really cool happened in a big football game! Two teams played, and it was full of cheers and super kicks! It was a day of amazing football fun!

The Big Game Day is Here!

Imagine a big green field. Lots and lots of people were watching the game. The sun was shining bright! Everyone was ready for some amazing football. It was a special game. It was part of something called the "World Cup." The World Cup is like the biggest football party ever for countries! So many teams want to win!

Meet the Teams!

One team was from a country called Spain. They wear red jerseys sometimes. Spain is a very strong team! They are known for playing very, very well. They are even called "European champions" sometimes. This means they are super good in their part of the world! They are a top team! The other team was from a country called Saudi Arabia. They also play football with great spirit! They try their very best. Both teams wanted to win this game very, very much. They worked hard and ran fast on the field. It was an important match for both teams.

Spain Scores Many Goals!

The game started! The players ran super fast! They passed the ball from one friend to another. They tried to get the ball into the goal. And guess what happened? The team from Spain scored not just one goal, but four goals! Wow! Four goals are a lot! It means Spain won the game 4 to 0. When a team scores zero goals, it means the other team played very strong defence. But Spain's players were just too good! They kept kicking the ball into the net. It was a big win for Spain! The fans cheered loudly for every goal!

Our Young Star: Lamine Yamal!

Now, let me tell you about a very special player. His name is Lamine Yamal. Lamine Yamal plays for Spain. He is quite young, maybe like a big brother to some of you! And guess what amazing thing he did? He scored a goal! It was his very first goal in this big World Cup journey. This is called "getting off the mark." It means he scored for the first time in this important competition. Can you imagine how happy he must have been? A big cheer for Lamine Yamal! He felt super proud!

What is the World Cup?

Did you know? The World Cup is a very famous football competition! It's the biggest football tournament in the world. Teams from all over the world play in it. They play to see who is the very best football team on Earth! It happens only every four years. So, it's a very special event! Our own African teams also work hard to play in the World Cup! We cheer for them!

Why This Game Was Important for Spain

Spain had played some games before this one. Sometimes, even super good teams don't always win every game. But in this game, Spain played super, super well! They scored many goals and showed everyone how strong they are. This big win helped them get "back on track" for the World Cup. It means they are now playing better! They feel more confident to win even more games! They are happy!

Fun Football Facts!

Did you know? A football field is also called a pitch! It's where all the action happens. Did you know? Football is sometimes called soccer in other parts of the world, like America! But it's the same fun game! Did you know? The fastest goal ever scored in a World Cup game was in just 11 seconds! Zoom! Football is a game that teaches us about teamwork. Everyone needs to play together, like good friends.

More About the Game!

The players ran up and down the field. They kicked the ball with their feet. They used their heads to hit the ball sometimes too! Wow! But never their hands, unless they are the special player called the goalkeeper! The goalkeeper's job is to stop the ball from going into the net. But Spain's players were very clever! They kept finding ways to score goals. It was a very exciting match for everyone watching in the stadium and at home!

African Dreams!

Here in Africa, we love football so much! Many kids like you dream of becoming a big football star one day. Watching games like this can inspire us all! Maybe one day, you will score a goal for your country in the World Cup! That would be amazing! Keep practicing your kicks and passes. Play with your friends and have fun!

What Happens Next?

Spain will keep playing more games. They want to try and reach the World Cup finals. Every game is a step towards winning the big golden trophy! We will keep watching to see how they do! We are excited for more football fun! Hooray for football and all the amazing players! It was a great day for Spain. And especially for young Lamine Yamal, who scored his first big goal!
summary: Spain's football team played a big game, winning 4-0 against Saudi Arabia, with young player Lamine Yamal scoring his very first goal in the World Cup journey.
whyMatters: This game was important because Spain played very well and scored many goals, helping them feel strong and ready for more games in the big World Cup tournament, showing everyone they are a super team!

originalTitle: What’s next in the Strait of Hormuz crisis?
title: Trouble on the Big Sea Road Far Away!
content:
Hello, amazing friends in Africa! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, ready to tell you about something important happening far, far away from our beautiful continent. Don't worry, it's not scary, but it's good to know what's happening in our big, connected world.

What is a Water Road?

Imagine a special path, but not on land – it's on the ocean! We call these "waterways" or "straits." Think of them like super important highways for big boats. These boats carry all sorts of things, like food, toys, clothes, and even the fuel that makes cars go and lights shine. It’s how countries share things with each other across the big blue sea. Sometimes, these water paths are very narrow, like a small gate or a narrow bridge on the water. This makes them even more special because almost all the boats going in a certain direction *have* to use that one path. Did you know? Africa has many important water roads too, like the Suez Canal, which big ships use to go between Europe and Asia without sailing all around our amazing continent! Water roads help connect the whole world.

Meet the Strait of Hormuz!

Far, far away from us, in a part of the world called the Middle East, there is one of these super-important water paths called the "Strait of Hormuz." Can you say that? Hormuz! It's a bit of a tricky name. It's a very narrow strip of water that connects a big ocean to a gulf, which is like a smaller sea. It's like a special doorway between two big water rooms. Many, many huge ships, called tankers, sail through the Strait of Hormuz every single day. They are like giant floating trucks, carrying important goods for countries all over the planet. Did you know? More than one-fifth of all the oil that countries use around the world travels through this very strait! That's like saying 20 out of every 100 buckets of oil pass through there. This oil helps make electricity and fuel for many things.

Countries Far Away: Iran, Israel, and Lebanon

Now, let's talk about some countries that are far from Africa. There's a country called Iran. They have soldiers, just like many countries do, to help keep their people and their country safe. Then there are two other countries, Israel and Lebanon. Sometimes, countries can have disagreements, just like friends sometimes have little arguments or tough times. It's never nice when people or countries fight. Recently, Israel and Lebanon had a very tough time, and there were some attacks. It means they had a big disagreement that led to fighting, which made many people very sad and worried. Iran, hearing about this fight and being concerned, decided to do something about it that affects the special water path, the Strait of Hormuz. They wanted to show how serious the situation was.

Blocking the Water Road!

Because of the big fight between Israel and Lebanon, Iran's soldiers said they would "close" the Strait of Hormuz. This is a very big decision for a country to make. What does "close" mean for a water path? It means they might stop big ships, especially the ones carrying oil and other goods, from sailing through the Strait for a while. Imagine if someone blocked the only road to your school or the market! It would be hard for everyone to get there, right? It's a bit like that for ships and countries that need the goods they carry. Iran says they did this because they were upset about what happened with Israel and Lebanon. They want the world to pay attention and find a peaceful solution to the problems there. Did you know? When countries close important pathways, it often makes other countries worry because it can stop important goods from moving around the world, which affects many people.

Why Is This a Big Deal?

If the Strait of Hormuz is blocked, it means all those giant ships carrying oil and other supplies can't go through easily. They might have to take a much longer way around, or simply not go at all. This is a big deal because many countries around the world need that oil and those supplies. We use oil to make cars drive, planes fly, and even to help make electricity for our homes and schools, keeping the lights on and our food cold. If there's less oil moving around, the price of oil can go up. Imagine if bananas suddenly became very, very expensive because the trucks carrying them couldn't reach the market! It's similar for oil. When oil becomes more expensive, it can make other things more expensive too. Like the fuel for the big trucks that bring food to your town, or the buses that take you to school, or even the things made in factories. Did you know? The world needs a lot of energy every day, and oil is one of the biggest ways we get that energy right now. Finding new, cleaner ways to power our world is a big challenge for scientists and inventors!

How Does This Affect Us in Africa?

You might be thinking, "This is happening so far away, how does it affect me in Africa?" Well, our world is like a big family! We are all connected in many ways, even if we are far apart. When something big happens in one part of the world, like the blocking of an important water road, it can sometimes have a ripple effect everywhere else, even here in Africa. If oil prices go up because of the Strait of Hormuz being blocked, it can make things like petrol for cars and diesel for generators a little more expensive in African countries. This is because we often buy oil from other parts of the world. This means that things that use fuel, like buses, taxis, and even some factories that make clothes or shoes, might have to charge a little more money for their services or products. This is how the world is connected! But don't worry too much! Grown-ups and leaders in our countries are always thinking about these things and working hard to keep things stable and fair for everyone. They want to make sure you have what you need. Did you know? Many African countries also produce oil and gas, and they play an important role in the world's energy story! We have many important resources right here on our continent.

What Happens Next?

Right now, grown-ups and leaders from many countries, including those who care about peace, are talking to each other. They are trying very hard to find a good way to solve this problem peacefully. They want to make sure the water road stays open so that all the important ships can keep sailing safely and deliver all their goods to the places that need them. Nobody wants more fighting or problems. Everyone wants peace and for people to get along, so that children like you can grow up in a happy and safe world, full of learning and playing. Did you know? Talking and finding solutions together is always the best way to solve problems, much better than fighting or blocking things! It takes patience and kindness, but it's always worth it. So, my dear friends, that's the story of the Strait of Hormuz for today. It's a reminder that even far-away events can show us how connected we all are. Let's always hope for peace and kindness in our big, beautiful world! Stay curious and keep learning!
summary: Soldiers from Iran have said they will stop ships from using a very important water path called the Strait of Hormuz because of fighting between other countries, Israel and Lebanon.
whyMatters: This is important because many ships carry oil and other important things through this water path, and if it's blocked, it can make things more expensive for everyone, even in Africa, because our world is connected.

originalTitle: US and Iran begin talks on initial peace deal in Switzerland
title: US and Iran begin talks on initial peace deal in Switzerland
content: The face-to-face meeting follows ongoing fighting in Lebanon and Iran's claim to have shut the Strait of Hormuz.

originalTitle: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
title: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
content: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

originalTitle: Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict
title: Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict
content: Abiy Ahmed's party retains its huge majority despite unrest in several parts of Ethiopia and tensions with its neighbours.

originalTitle: The beauty of sharing your child's first World Cup
title: The beauty of sharing your child's first World Cup
content: There is nothing like the first World Cup you can enjoy with your kid - and the wide-eyed wonder through which they see it.

originalTitle: The World Cup records that look set to be broken
title: The World Cup records that look set to be broken
content: The 2026 World Cup may only be 10 days old but the tournament has already rewritten football history. BBC Sport takes a look at the records already broken, or set to be.

originalTitle: What have we learned from Scotland's World Cup so far?
title: What have we learned from Scotland's World Cup so far?
content: After a win over Haiti and a narrow defeat by Morocco, BBC Sport Scotland assesses what we have learned from Scotland's World Cup so far.
